# Spec — 2026-05-12

## Executive Summary

- **anthropics/claude-code** (v2.1.139): Major release — `/goal` command for multi-turn autonomous goal completion with live overlay (elapsed/turns/tokens); Agent View (`claude agents`) for managing all sessions; hook `args: string[]` exec-form (no shell needed); hook `continueOnBlock` for PostToolUse rejection-as-feedback loops; compaction prompt now preserves sensitive user instructions; MCP stdio servers receive `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`; subagent API requests carry agent-id tracing headers via OTEL
- **Kilo-Org/kilocode** (v7.2.50–v7.2.52, ~20 commits): Tool output pruning for large requests; IDN/punycode normalization in permission dialogs; removal of `--dangerously-skip-permissions`; nested subagent delegation prevention; default model changed to `kilo-auto/free`; model info panel with modality/description
- **cline/cline** (~40 commits this week): CLI compaction mode setting with basic/agentic strategies; MCP OAuth support; MCP server settings toggles; SDK monorepo merge; kanban command and notice dialog; `.cline` workflow config paths; TypeScript plugin type-only imports; provider grouping by popularity; Exa web search plugin
- **aider-ai/aider**: No new commits in last 7 days (latest: Apr 25). Previously reported features (Claude 4.7/4.6, GPT-5.5, ExInfo) remain the most recent
- **continuedev/continue**: No new commits in last 7 days (latest: Apr 17). Previously reported features (Mercury-2, dynamic model fetching, session filtering) remain the most recent
- **Key new gap**: Claude Code's `/goal` command — set a completion condition and the agent works autonomously across turns until met. We have no equivalent "goal-directed autonomous mode" in our orchestrator
- **Key new gap**: Claude Code's hook `continueOnBlock` — when a PostToolUse hook rejects execution, the rejection reason is fed back to the model to self-correct. Our hooks system fires events but has no rejection-feedback loop
- **Pattern trend**: Compaction quality is now a differentiator — both Claude Code (preserve sensitive instructions) and Cline (basic vs agentic strategies) shipped compaction improvements. Our compaction uses a generic summary prompt without instruction-preservation or strategy selection
- **Pattern trend**: Agent observability headers — Claude Code now injects `x-claude-code-agent-id` / `x-claude-code-parent-agent-id` into API requests and OTEL spans. Useful for debugging multi-agent orchestrations, which we support but don't trace
- **Convergence signal**: MCP OAuth support appeared in Cline this week (same pattern Claude Code fixed last week). Our MCP client only supports stdio transport; no OAuth/SSE support

## What to build (prioritized)

### 1. Goal-Directed Autonomous Mode
- **What**: Add a `/goal` command that accepts a completion condition, then orchestrates multi-turn agent loops until the condition is satisfied, showing live progress (turns, tokens, elapsed time)
- **Why**: Claude Code's `/goal` enables hands-off task completion — users set a target and walk away. Our orchestrator runs single-turn or requires manual re-prompting. This is the highest-impact UX gap for power users running long tasks
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 — "Added `/goal` command: set a completion condition and Claude keeps working across turns until it's met"
- **Category**: command
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 2. Compaction Instruction Preservation
- **What**: Enhance compaction prompt to explicitly preserve sensitive user instructions (API keys mentioned, coding style preferences, project constraints) and add strategy selection (basic truncation vs. agentic context-aware summarization)
- **Why**: Current compaction uses a generic summary prompt. Claude Code now explicitly asks the model to preserve sensitive user instructions during compaction. Cline added basic/agentic strategy selection. Users lose important context after compaction
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 — "Compaction prompt now asks the model to preserve sensitive user instructions"; cline/cline #66 — "compaction: correctness pass for basic and agentic strategies"
- **Category**: core
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 3. Hook Rejection Feedback Loop (continueOnBlock)
- **What**: Add `continueOnBlock` option to PostToolUse hooks — when a hook rejects a tool execution, feed the rejection reason back to the model as an error message so it can self-correct instead of halting
- **Why**: Claude Code's `continueOnBlock` enables guardrail hooks that guide the model rather than just blocking it. Our hooks fire-and-forget with no model feedback path. This enables patterns like "don't write to production DB" → model retries with test DB
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 — "Added hook `continueOnBlock` config option for PostToolUse — set to true to feed the hook's rejection reason back to Claude and continue the turn"
- **Category**: config
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 4. MCP Project Directory Environment Variable
- **What**: Pass `ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR` (or equivalent) to MCP stdio server child processes, matching the project root detection used by hooks
- **Why**: MCP servers often need to know the project directory to resolve relative paths. Claude Code now passes `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` to all stdio MCP servers. Our MCP client spawns servers without project context, forcing users to hardcode paths in MCP configs
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 — "MCP stdio servers now receive `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` in their environment"
- **Category**: mcp
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 5. Agent Observability Headers (OTEL Tracing)
- **What**: When making LLM API requests from subagents, inject `x-alexi-agent-id` and `x-alexi-parent-agent-id` headers plus corresponding OpenTelemetry span attributes for distributed tracing of multi-agent orchestrations
- **Why**: Multi-agent debugging is opaque without request-level tracing. Claude Code now traces all subagent API calls with agent hierarchy headers. Our task/agent system makes API calls without identifying which agent in the hierarchy originated them
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 — "API requests from subagents now carry `x-claude-code-agent-id` / `x-claude-code-parent-agent-id` headers, and OTEL spans include `agent_id` / `parent_agent_id` attributes"
- **Category**: provider
- **Size**: small (< 4h)
